NXT-63753.summary: FH - FRE Apps - SSN question for Students at CEP Sites
NXT-63753.rn_title: SSN Entry for Online Combo Forms at CEP Sites
NXT-63753.ac:
* System Setting adjusted
* If setting set to Partial:
** Verify: FRE Apps with all students at CEP Sites do not see the Last-4 SSN / No SSN question.
** Verify: FRE Image App available to the applicant does not show the Last-4 SSN / No SSN question.
** Verify: Eligibility module shows ‘No SSN or Not Applicable’
NXT-63753.rn:
We have made a small update to allow for a unique scenario that some customers, primarily those in Colorado, are beginning to run into. The scenario is aimed at the submission of “Combo Forms” that serve as both an FRE Application and an Income Survey used for additional funds.

For this scenario, Income Surveys are turned off, and FRE Applications are being exclusively used to serve as a Combo Form. As a reminder, Income Surveys are managed by a separate setting (*Enable Income Surveys* - Code: ADDINCSUR). If set to No, then an FRE Application will be used for all online submissions.

The new functionality introduced here is for a scenario where *all students on an application are at a CEP site*. For this scenario the applicant does not need to respond to a question about their Last-4 / No SSN.

To achieve this, we have added a new option to the previously introduced setting: *FRE Applications - Applicant SSN Question Required* (Code: FRENOSSN).

Set this to ‘Partial’ if you want applicants with all students at a CEP school to not see or respond to the Last-4 SSN question.
NXT-63753.desc:
*requirements*

* Update System Setting (FRENOSSN)
** Dropdown: On, Off (Default), Partial
** Description: Controls whether the applicant must answer / submit last-4 SSN as part of an online FRE application. Set this to Yes if this is mandatory for your state, or No to remove the question from the household application workflow. Set to Partial to remove the question if all students are at CEP schools.
* Add in a check for FRE Applications via Family Hub:
** All students on the FRE app are at CEP Sites
** Do not show / require the applicant to answer the Last-4 SSN / No SSN.
* Eligibility module:
** Text tied to No SSN should reflect as the alternate ‘No SSN or Not Applicable’ string.

* User can see Status and Eligibility in the Match Details screen 
* Once Matches are processed:
** student is linked to the app
** student eligibility updated accordingly
NXT-66798.rn:
We have made some updates to how pending students are shown and selected for FRE Application forms.

* *Match* buttons are available if multiple students are available to link against the Pending student.
* Application Details are only updated after *Process Matches* is clicked.
* Status and Eligibility have been added to the Match Details screen for improved clarity.
NXT-66798.desc:
!image-20250923-190330.png|width=466,alt="image-20250923-190330.png"!

*requirements*

* User should be able to select 1 of the matches and complete the student matching.
* User should be able to see if one of the potential accounts is Active/Inactive
** Add the following lines for Student Details:
*** Status
**** Show Active or Inactive
*** Eligibility
**** Show Free/Reduced/Paid and Basis
***** Example: Free (DC MEDICAID)

----

SCOPE CREEP 10/9/25

* Workflow changes for the following scenarios:
** System single matches.
** System multiple matches.
** Manual matches.
* All scenarios link a Pending Student to an actual Account (personid)
** We need to track the link.
** Application Details should *not* be updated as part of the search / manual matching.
* User needs to click Process Matches: 
** to have the Application Details update.
** Existing workflow continues from here - student receives benefits, etc.
* Unmatch / Match button available if more than 1 potential pending student is found / added.
* Add the following strings:
** On the page itself:
*** You can see pending students from applications on this page. The *Find Matches* button will attempt to locate students to match them against. You can find, edit, and review matches under the Actions column. Find matches first, and then use the select checkboxes and the *Process Matches* button to extend eligibility.

*** You are viewing the submitted information from the Form and details from at least 1 potential student. If multiple students are shown, select one with the *Match* button to proceed.

NXT-66821.summary: Elig - DC - Extensions - Extending a different DC Type to the same student, blocking inactive Source students
NXT-66821.rn_title: Small Changes to Direct Certification Extensions
NXT-66821.ac:
* User can perform a DC Extension repeatedly between the same students. (scenario A)
** Manual Match should be possible in this scenario, but the user is advised accordingly.
* Inactive students are not used as a source of DC Extension + data clean-up. (scenario B)
NXT-66821.rn:
We have made a couple small changes to how direct certification extensions are handled.

# Inactive students will no longer be shown as available source students in the Search & Review tab to extend benefits from.
# The same students can be repeatedly matched via the Manual Match option as long as the new Direct Cert. type is higher on the precedence order.
NXT-66821.desc:
[https://primeroedge.freshdesk.com/a/tickets/295727|https://primeroedge.freshdesk.com/a/tickets/295727] - trying to extend SNAP after having previously extended Medicaid

[https://primeroedge.freshdesk.com/a/tickets/296439|https://primeroedge.freshdesk.com/a/tickets/296439] - same scenario 

[https://primeroedge.freshdesk.com/a/tickets/314274|https://primeroedge.freshdesk.com/a/tickets/314274] - scenario B below

*requirements*

Scenario A:

* User can perform a DC Extension between two students repeatedly:
** Only if the DC Type has changed.
*** Example: Student A (DC Medicaid), Student B (Paid)
*** Extension from A → B (DC Medicaid)
*** Student A updates to DC SNAP
*** Extension from A → B (DC SNAP)
* User can perform a Manual Match repeatedly along the same lines
* -User is shown warning text if the two students have been previously matched but is not blocked like they are currently.-
** -Warning text: This extension has happened previously and can be seen under Reviewed. You can match them again if required.-



!image-20250923-204326.png|width=465,alt="image-20250923-204326.png"!

----

[https://primeroedge.freshdesk.com/a/tickets/314274|https://primeroedge.freshdesk.com/a/tickets/314274]

Scenario B

* DC Extension is created for a student who is active (Student A → Student B)
* Potential Extension is not reviewed 
* Student A goes Inactive
* Potential Extensions continue to be created because the student was active with DC benefits at one point

*requirements*

* Check DC Extensions for Inactive Source Students (the one with benefits) and delete/block any potential extensions from being created on them.
** User can then use Manual to do an extension on them if required.



*Discussion Notes* {{2026-04-10}} with Hussain and Harsha.

* Scope on this is to be limited to Manual Match only for Scenario A.
* Scope on Scenario B is for only inactive students.

NXT-70380.summary: Elig./FH - Income Surveys - Add New Setting to stop auto-processing Surveys with Unidentified Students
NXT-70380.rn_title: Customize when to Automatically Process Income Surveys
NXT-70380.ac:
Verify new setting has been added and each value functions as described above

Verify manual entry behavior is unchanged
NXT-70380.rn: A new system setting has been added, *_Auto-Process all Income Surveys_*, which will allow greater flexibility over if and when to automatically process a survey that has been submitted online.
NXT-70380.desc:
{panel:bgColor=#deebff}
Carrying forward work completed as part of [https://dev.azure.com/Cybersoft-Technologies-Inc/PrimeroEdge%20Classic/_workitems/edit/99540/|https://dev.azure.com/Cybersoft-Technologies-Inc/PrimeroEdge%20Classic/_workitems/edit/99540/] 
{panel}



Ticket: [https://primeroedge.freshdesk.com/a/tickets/267487|https://primeroedge.freshdesk.com/a/tickets/267487] 

*Summary:*

* Currently, surveys without identified students (students with missing required info like IDs) are still being auto-processed under certain conditions. Though the student does get marked as being pending.

*Details:*

* Add a new setting to correct this.
** Setting Name: *_Auto-Process all Online Income Surveys_* 
** Description: This setting determines when a manually-entered income survey can be automatically processed by the system.
** Options: Yes/No/Only if all students identified
*** Default Setting value: *Only if all students identified*
*** When setting value is ‘_Yes’_, there is no change in functionality (continue the current functionality of processing all income surveys)
*** When set to ‘_No’_, do not auto-process any surveys (surveys will always have the status of ‘Pending’)
*** When set to ‘_Only if all students identified’_, only process income surveys where all students on income survey are matched/identified.
** Manual entry of income surveys is unchanged.

NXT-71226.summary: Eligibility - Other Forms - (Part 1) Complete 'Add New Form Application' button functionality
NXT-71226.ac: Verify that the ‘Add New Form Application’ button is now functional, allowing the user to select their form of choice and then fill out (but not submit) an application according to that form’s configuration.
NXT-71226.desc:
As a Cybersoft Implementation team member or district admin, I would like to be able to add Summer EBT (as well as any other non-traditional form) applications at a district-level with minimal steps, so that I do not have to use an external system just for this purpose.

*Summary*

* Allow users to add a form application for any active form type 
** *Story 1 (Application button/page UI, API): Effort - 8*
** Story 2 (Submit/Pending and Application Image): Effort - 8

*Details*

* In Eligibility > Forms > Other Forms, enable the ‘Add New Form Application’ button to open a stepper which mimics the Add New Survey page for current Income Surveys.
** This new entry page can be titled: *Add New Form Application*.
**  It would be great if somewhere on the page, or even on each step, if we can show the name of the specific form name to which this application belongs.
* This button will need to allow users to select +one+ active form type (i.e. Summer EBT form, Income Survey, ‘Form ABC’, etc.).
** Design is negotiable; i.e. currently this button opens a drawer. If we include the active forms in that drawer and have the user pick one which then takes them to the ‘Add New Form Application’ page for that form type, that is one solution.
* Within the ‘Form Application Entry’ page, have the stepper contain all of the steps of the existing application, with the exception of those functions which are tied to that form’s configuration. These steps will change based on the options selected:
* Income collection method
** Choice: *Individual / Household / None*
** Behavior: In the Household step of the application, we should display the related income collection fields according to what was chosen above
*** Individual: display the Household step with the individual household members having their own income (similar to current FRE app)
**** Only in this scenario, we will collect PFD
*** Household: display the Household step with a single total household income field (similar to current Income Survey)
*** None: hide the income fields entirely. Only Total Household Members/Household Size should remain.
* Contact Info Step
** Require First and Last Name fields only
* Form requires last 4 digits of SSN
** Choice: *Yes/No/Partial*
** Behavior: In the Comments step of the application, show or hide all text and fields for asking about/collecting SSN. If ‘Yes’, display a ‘Last 4 digits of SSN’ checkbox and field beneath the signature line (copy design from the FRE application stepper). 


!image-20260318-034951.png|width=671,alt="image-20260318-034951.png"!

!image-20260318-035018.png|width=719,alt="image-20260318-035018.png"!



* …
** Display it on the Summary step as well.
** Treat ‘Partial’ SSN the same as ‘Yes’
* Form requires electronic signature
** Choice: *Yes/No*
** Behavior: In the Submit step of the application, show or hide signature field/selection. 
* Allow the user to submit the application as complete or mark it as ‘Pending’, as well as the same ‘I’m Done’ or ‘Submit Another Form Application’ options. We can display submitted forms in the next story, and ‘Qualified’/’Unqualified’ results in a separate story as well.
** Add ‘Pending Reasons’ (same as Applications) as well, plus the related logic. i.e. household size needs to match or otherwise users cannot submit.
* Additionally, at the end of the application, display the associated form application image with its associated print/export buttons.



Reference screens

NXT-71694.summary: Elig - DC - Split Street Address for DC Matching
NXT-71694.rn_title: Address field split for Direct Certification Matching
NXT-71694.ac:
* Street Address split
* DC Matching algorithm updated
* DC Scorecard updated
* Prior Scorecards not impacted
* User can adjust points from the DC Match Configuration page
NXT-71694.rn:
We have made an update to how the full street address is handled for direct certification matching. Previously, it was comparing the full street address - now, it is split into the individual fields. Street Address, City, State, and Zip. This will help gain a few more points in scenarios where 1 wrong part of the address was blocking point allocation. 

For example, take the address *742 Evergreen Terrace, Springfield, MO.* This entire address had to match between the Student Data and the Direct Cert file for points to be awarded.

But if we had *742 Evergreen Terrace, Shelbyville, MO*. instead, we’ll see points awarded for Street Address and State. 

We’re hopeful this will assist with more automatic matches in the upcoming school year!
NXT-71694.desc:
As Dana the Director I want to better control how Street Address is matching on the DC Scorecard to catch scenarios where points are not allocated due to City/Town being different in some parts.

!image-20260406-171548.png|width=1024,alt="image-20260406-171548.png"!

*requirements*

* Split Street Address (20 pts) up into:
** Street Address (14 pts)
** City (2 pt)
** State (2 pt) 
** Zip Code (2 pt)
* Update DC Matching to make use of the individual fields and the new default points
* Update the DC Scorecard to show the individual fields
* User should be able to see prior DC Scorecards with no issue - if we matched on the “full” Street Address and gave 20 pts, show that

NXT-71823.summary: Eligibility - Configuration - FRE Application Image - Add Date Range to bind an image to a subset of Apps
NXT-71823.ac:
Verify that a user can associate any FRE app image with a date range

Verify that if there is only the Standard Template, we will always use that. Whenever a new image is created, it will automatically use that. Past templates cannot be edited. When 2 custom templates are created, the previous one will be inactive and not display in the grid.

Verify that date ranges for images cannot overlap with each other (will be set in back end)

Verify that History is maintained for date range changes

Verify that the page description/note at the top of the page has been updated

Verify that when a application is opened, we check to see if its Received Date is within a particular range and then display the specific application image

Verify that this is the case in both SchoolCafé and the Family Hub

Verify that the standard FRE application image now only applies to new applications (+*if*+ Status column is maintained). Otherwise, verify that Status column is removed
NXT-71823.desc:
As a Cybersoft admin, I would like to be able to modify the HTML image template for my FRE apps and have it be for a certain time period, so that I may make them more closely aligned to my SchoolCafé (and paper) application.

*Summary*

Instead of only displaying one FRE image for all applications past and present, add a ‘Date Range’ column which controls when a specific image will be the one which is visible to users in both SchoolCafé and the Family Hub. Make sure dates do not overlap. This will be a cleaner solution to help prevent potential audit issues.

*Details*

* In _Eligibility > Settings > Application Image_: 
** add a new column to allow users to designate a date range for an application image template for the current FRE Application. 
*** When a user opens an FRE application in either SchoolCafé or the Family Hub, open the image that corresponds to that date (allowing older apps to display an older app image)
*** This field will now be required (we will set it in the background)
*** We should update the History to capture date range changes as well
** remove the current ‘Status’ column, since display will be by data range now. 
** update the page note at the top *from*:
*** _Please make sure that_ +_any_+ _changes to this image are in compliance with the rules and regulations for the state to which this district belongs, and be advised that updates made to this image will immediately impact ALL applications,_ +_past and present_+_._

***to:*

*** _Please make sure that_ +_any_+ _changes to application images are in compliance with the rules and regulations for the state to which this district belongs, and be advised that updates made to this image will take immediate effect._

* When opening an application, open the correct application image based on the provided date range (application’s Received Date).
* Automatically handle marking an existing application image as ‘closed’ when a new one is edited.

This story is complete when a user can: 

* create a new form that is configured to have its own notification letters, then see the new letter type added for that form in (if possible) multiple languages

* click that button and have the Active template filled out with details related to that applicant's information

NXT-72802.desc:
*Summary*

When the user adds a form that is configured to have its own letter templates, add a new letter template type: {{Form}} Application Notification, and have it be sent when a user clicks the button to Notify an applicant after Validation of the form.

*Details*

* When a user creates a new Form type in Forms Configuration, automatically create a new letter type in the default languages (at least English and Spanish): {{Form}} Application Notices.
** Related screen:
** NOTE: One of the questions when creating a new Form is if it will have its own notification letters. I think it would be good to create them every time anyway and just hide them unless and until the user indicates a 'Yes' to this question.
** For the Template Name, get the form description from the form itself. Ex. "*CybersoftForm Application Notice*" or "*PrimeroForm Application Notice*". The district can update these via 'Save As' later if needed.

!image-20260512-193545.png|width=1035,alt="image-20260512-193545.png"!


** For the content, these letter templates can be a copy of our existing Summer EBT letter template, the "Program Application Notice" except for the name of the template itself. If we don’t have this type in the DB, use this one: [^DefaultApprovalLetterENG.html] 
** In the Templates page:
*** Do not allow ‘Save As’
*** Do not allow user to make a letter inactive
**** If the user disables the Form OR says ‘No’ to the notification letter question, make the template inactive that way
** {color:#6554c0}*Daimien will add Table/Letter fields before taking up the story.*{color}


{color:#6554c0}*This will be in a separate story:*{color}

* When a user opens a form application's details, add a 'Notify' button. 
** Only display/enable this button if the form is configured to have its own notification letters.
** When clicked, open the *Active* '{{Form}} Application Notice' template for that form with the details filled in based on the selected application.
** Allow the user to print the email or email it to the email address on file if one has been provided (follow the same behavior as the FRE applications)
* When a letter has been printed/emailed, add a historical records in the forms' Letter History as well.
** We will want to verify this displays on the students' record so that it is visible in the Notification History within SchoolCafé as well. (May be in a separate story)

* *This story is complete when a user can:* 
** create a new form that is configured to have its own notification letters, then see the new letter type added for that form in (if possible) multiple languages 
** add/process an application using that form type and see a 'Notify' button in the application details
*** click that button and have the Active template filled out with details related to that applicant's information
*** print and email the notification has needed
** see a record of that printed/emailed notification has been saved

* *Will likely be in a separate story:*
** User will eventually be able to view that notification in SchoolCafé family hub when adding that student to a parent's account
** Disabling editing of Form Configuration fields after at least one application has been submitted using that Form type. Otherwise there could be a scenario where an application could not be notified.
*** At that time, we can also add a tooltip to show why certain details on the Form can no longer be edited.

NXT-70916.desc:
Build out central-level and district-level features and functions for administering statewide direct certification. 

+*Central Features and Functions:*+

* DC file management with list of files per DC program type
* DC data processing and distribution (process and segment data to each region from larger file)
* View DC stats/report per file (breakdown with data visuals and list of district matching stats in the table)
* Monitor district usage (how many times accessed, downloaded matches, and uploaded SIS if applicable) and FNS834 report - possible KPI for Insights
* Use cases: 
** Basic – Ability to just upload a DC file, distribute raw data per district, provide access to download raw information (not common but possible). Each district responsible for uploading into their systems. 
** Standard - Ability to just upload a DC file, perform matching (assumes SIS is available), upload SIS state or district-level), provide tools for potential and unmatched, distribute matched data per district, provide access to download matched and/or unmatched information (most common). 
** Advanced - Same as standard but we detect that the district is using SchoolCafe eligibility and these processes are automated and directly update the local districts information. These would then do their normal work of notifications as well. 
* Settings, configuration, matching scoring, DC file configuration/mapping, SIS file configuration/mapping 
* Integration for statewide SIS uploads and/or API integration 
* KPIs
** Expand the Eco Dis KPI to include eligibility programs/categories (DC, Apps, etc by status of F, R, P) 
** Show DC matching requirements (at least 3 times per year)
* Feature enablement
** List of Districts and their schools with basic configuration
** Set/view provisions, programs operating, etc. 
** Be able to separate those districts who are using the district “full suite” products vs those who are not
** Gives flexibility outside of just the licenses.
* Licensing and access based on program use
** Remove access from DC if the district is not using those programs.
** Option with the district and site program configuration to enable to include/exclude a district for the current academic year.

NXT-70918.desc:
Build out central-level and district-level features and functions for administering statewide direct certification. 

+*District Features and Functions:*+

* Use cases:
** Basic – Ability to just upload a DC file, distribute raw data per district, provide access to download raw information (not common but possible). Each district responsible for uploading into their systems. 
** Standard - Ability to just upload a DC file, perform matching (assumes SIS is available), upload SIS state or district-level), provide tools for potential and unmatched, distribute matched data per district, provide access to download matched and/or unmatched information (most common). 
** Advanced - Same as standard but we detect that the district is using SchoolCafe eligibility and these processes are automated and directly update the local districts information. These would then do their normal work of notifications as well. 
* District only views and experience
** DC Files (View what was imported by central)
*** View DC matching summary and consolidated raw, matched, and/or unmatched data for exporting
*** View DC files uploaded by the state with total matches and ability to download raw data, matched data, and/or unmatched data
*** View DC file matching detail stats/report
*** View DC raw file data for those not performing matches but want access to their data (need a way to indicate that files were downloaded and matched in the local system)
** DC Extract (View/export as a district)
*** Common file format for exporting raw, matched, and/or unmatched DC data
** Student Files (View/upload as a district)
*** Upload SIS files for matching (if statewide files not in use) with preset format
*** View SIS file upload history with matches triggered
** DC compliance
*** View my compliance stats
*** View my FNS834 report 
** Matched (setting enabled - standard/advanced)
** Potential Matched (setting enabled - standard/advanced)
** Extensions (setting enabled - standard/advanced)
** Unmatched (setting enabled - basic/standard/advanced)
** File Search (setting enabled - standard/advanced)
** Notifications (setting enable - standard/advanced)
